verses.1: For the music director. A Psalm of David. Hear my voice, God, in my complaint; Protect my life from dread of the enemy.
verses.2: Hide me from the secret discussion of evildoers, From the restlessness of the workers of injustice,
verses.3: Who have sharpened their tongues like a sword. They aimed bitter speech as their arrows,
verses.4: To shoot from concealment at the innocent; Suddenly they shoot at him, and do not fear.
verses.5: They make firm for themselves an evil purpose; They talk of setting snares secretly; They say, “Who can see them?”
verses.6: They devise injustices, saying, “We are ready with a well-conceived plot”; For the inward thought and the heart of a person are deep.
verses.7: But God will shoot an arrow at them; Suddenly they will be wounded.
verses.8: So they will make him stumble; Their own tongue is against them; All who see them will shake their heads.
verses.9: Then all people will fear, And they will declare the work of God, And will consider what He has done.
verses.10: The righteous person will be glad in the LORD and take refuge in Him; And all the upright in heart will boast.
